title: QRverse- Component 1 Text QR Code Generator

content.problemStatement:
Welcome to **QRverse**, your journey into the world of QR code generation using Python! In this first component, you'll learn how to turn any piece of text — like a name, message, or secret — into a scannable QR code. QR codes are used everywhere — from restaurant menus to digital passes — and you'll create your very own version right now.

---

**Problem Statement**

Your task is to **build a simple Python program** that generates a **QR code from user input text**.

- Prompt the user to **enter any text** (it can be a name, sentence, or anything).
- Use the `qrcode` library to generate a QR code from this input.
- Save the QR code as an image file (e.g., `text_qr.png`) using `Pillow` library.
- After saving, print a success message to the user.
 
**[qrcode](https://pypi.org/project/qrcode/) and [Pillow](https://pypi.org/project/pillow/) libraries are **already installed on the platform** — no need to install anything!**


---

**Example**

**Input:**

```sdfs
Enter text to encode: Hello from QRverse!
```

**Output:**
```sdfs
QR code saved as 'text_qr.png'
````

---

**When scanned, this QR will display: `Hello from QRverse!`**
constraints:
- $1 \leq T \leq 100$
- $1 \leq N \leq 100$
- $1 \leq M \leq N\cdot(N-1)/2$
- $1 \leq u_i, v_i \leq N$
- $u_i \neq v_i$ for each $1 \leq i \leq M$.
- The sum of $N$ over all test cases won't exceed $100$.
content.inputFormat:
- The first line of input will contain a single integer $T$, denoting the number of test cases.
- Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.
    - The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers $N$ and $M$ — the number of vertices and edges of the graph, respectively.
    - The next $M$ lines describe the edges. The $i$-th of these $M$ lines contains two space-separated integers $u_i$ and $v_i$, denoting an edge between $u_i$ and $v_i$.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output on a new line the number of good subgraphs of $G$, modulo $10^9 + 7$.
